Write a Rail program that sorts [5,1,4,2,3] using insertion sort and prints the head

Output ONLY the Rail code.

insert x xs =
  if length xs == 0 then [x]
  else if x <= head xs then cons x xs
  else cons (head xs) (insert x (tail xs))

isort xs =
  if length xs == 0 then []
  else insert (head xs) (isort (tail xs))

main =
  let sorted = isort [5,1,4,2,3]
  let _ = print (show (head sorted))
  0

You are a Rail language expert. Rail is a pure functional language that compiles to native ARM64. Write correct Rail code.

Write a Rail program with type Op = | Push x | Mul | Halt, execute [Push 6, Push 7, Mul, Halt] on a stack, print the top

Output ONLY the Rail code.

type Op = | Push x | Mul | Halt

exec ops stack =
  if length ops == 0 then stack
  else
    let op = head ops
    let rest = tail ops
    match op
      | Push x -> exec rest (cons x stack)
      | Mul -> exec rest (cons (head stack * head (tail stack)) (tail (tail stack)))
      | Halt -> stack

main =
  let result = exec [Push 6, Push 7, Mul, Halt] []
  let _ = print (show (head result))
  0
You are a Rail language expert. Rail is a pure functional language that compiles to native ARM64. Write correct Rail code.

Write a Rail program with a stack machine that pushes 3 and 4, adds them, prints 7

Output ONLY the Rail code.

type Op = | Push x | Add | Halt

exec ops stack =
  if length ops == 0 then stack
  else
    let op = head ops
    let rest = tail ops
    match op
      | Push x -> exec rest (cons x stack)
      | Add -> exec rest (cons (head stack + head (tail stack)) (tail (tail stack)))
      | Halt -> stack

main =
  let result = exec [Push 3, Push 4, Add, Halt] []
  let _ = print (show (head result))
  0

Write a Rail program with type Expr = | Num x | Mul a b, evaluate Mul (Num 6) (Num 7), print the value

Output ONLY the Rail code.

type Expr = | Num x | Mul a b

eval_expr e = match e
  | Num x -> x
  | Mul a b -> eval_expr a * eval_expr b

main =
  let _ = print (show (eval_expr (Mul (Num 6) (Num 7))))
  0

Write a Rail program with Expr ADT supporting Num, Add, and Mul, evaluate Mul (Add (Num 2) (Num 3)) (Num 4)

Output ONLY the Rail code.

type Expr = | Num x | Add a b | Mul a b

eval e = match e
  | Num x -> x
  | Add a b -> eval a + eval b
  | Mul a b -> eval a * eval b

main =
  let _ = print (show (eval (Mul (Add (Num 2) (Num 3)) (Num 4))))
  0

Write a Rail program that writes 3 JSONL lines, filters for lines containing error, prints count

Output ONLY the Rail code.

count_matches xs s =
  if length xs == 0 then 0
  else if str_contains s (head xs) then 1 + count_matches (tail xs) s
  else count_matches (tail xs) s

not_empty s = length s > 0

main =
  let _ = write_file "/tmp/jl.txt" "{"a":1}
{"b":2,"error":true}
{"c":3}
"
  let lines = filter not_empty (split "
" (read_file "/tmp/jl.txt"))
  let _ = print (show (count_matches lines "error"))
  0

Write a Rail program that writes score=70, reads it, adds 5, writes score=75, reads and prints

Output ONLY the Rail code.

parse_int_acc cs acc =
  if length cs == 0 then acc
  else
    let c = head cs
    let d = if c == "0" then 0 else if c == "1" then 1 else if c == "2" then 2 else if c == "3" then 3 else if c == "4" then 4 else if c == "5" then 5 else if c == "6" then 6 else if c == "7" then 7 else if c == "8" then 8 else if c == "9" then 9 else 0
    parse_int_acc (tail cs) (acc * 10 + d)

parse_int s = parse_int_acc (chars s) 0

main =
  let _ = write_file "/tmp/score.txt" "score=70"
  let content = read_file "/tmp/score.txt"
  let val = parse_int (head (tail (split "=" content)))
  let new_val = val + 5
  let _ = write_file "/tmp/score.txt" (append "score=" (show new_val))
  let _ = print (read_file "/tmp/score.txt")
  0

Write a Rail program that writes k1=v1 and k2=v2, finds k2 line, prints value

Output ONLY the Rail code.

not_empty s = length s > 0

find_line xs prefix =
  if length xs == 0 then ""
  else if str_contains prefix (head xs) then head xs
  else find_line (tail xs) prefix

main =
  let _ = write_file "/tmp/kv.txt" "k1=v1
k2=v2"
  let lines = filter not_empty (split "
" (read_file "/tmp/kv.txt"))
  let line = find_line lines "k2"
  let _ = print (head (tail (split "=" line)))
  0

Write a Rail program with find_key that reads key=val pairs and prints the value for name

  else if str_contains key (head lines) then head (tail (split "=" (head lines)))
  else find_key (tail lines) key

main =
  let _ = write_file "/tmp/kv2.txt" "name=rail
version=1
"
  let lines = filter not_empty (split "
" (read_file "/tmp/kv2.txt"))

Write a Rail program with type State = | Init | Run | Done, transition Init to Done, print Done

Output ONLY the Rail code.

type State = | Init | Run | Done

next s = match s
  | Init -> Run
  | Run -> Done
  | Done -> Done

show_state s = match s
  | Init -> "Init"
  | Run -> "Run"
  | Done -> "Done"

main =
  let s = next (next Init)
  let _ = print (show_state s)
  0
